§ 01Transaction Closed

Dear {{cpaName}},

The ESOP transaction for {{companyName}} closed on {{closingDate}}. This package provides the tax-relevant information your firm will need to prepare the seller's and company's tax returns for the year of the sale.

Closed
ESOP Transaction Closed
{{closingDate}}
100% ESOP — new plan
{{entityTypeAtClosing}}

§ 03§1042 Election Status
{{election1042Status}}
{{qrsReinvestmentAmount}}
{{gainDeferred}}
{{gainRecognized}}
{{reinvestmentDeadline}}
§1042 election reminder: The election must be made on the seller's federal income tax return for the year of sale. It is irrevocable once made. If the seller reinvested less than 100% of the gain, the election applies proportionally. The basis in the QRS is reduced by the amount of gain deferred.
§ 04Enclosed Documents

The following documents are enclosed with this package:

A.Stock Purchase Agreement. Executed copy showing the final terms, purchase price, and closing conditions.
B.Closing statement / settlement statement. Itemized breakdown of all funds transferred at closing.
C.Seller note terms. If applicable — principal, interest rate, amortization schedule, and subordination terms.
D.Wire transfer confirmations. Confirmation of all wires sent at closing.
E.ESOP trust agreement. Executed copy of the ESOP trust agreement.
F.Entity conversion documents. If applicable — articles of conversion, tax election forms (check-the-box), and state filings.
G.Appraisal summary. Executive summary of the independent FMV appraisal (full report available upon request from the trustee).
§ 05Key Tax Filing Items

Items for CPA attention in preparing the year-of-sale returns:

1.Seller's personal return (1040). Report the capital gain on Schedule D. If §1042 election is made, report the deferred gain and attach the election statement. If installment sale treatment applies, file Form 6252.
2.Company's corporate return (1120). The company is now a C-Corporation with an ESOP. Report the ESOP contribution deduction, loan interest deduction, and any entity conversion implications.
3.ESOP Form 5500. The ESOP trust must file Form 5500 annually. The first filing covers the short plan year from the closing date to December 31. Coordinate with the ESOP administrator.
4.State returns. Verify state conformity to §1042. Some states do not allow the deferral. Report the gain (or deferred gain) on the seller's state return accordingly.
5.Estimated tax payments. If the seller has a significant recognized gain, estimated tax payments may be required to avoid underpayment penalties. Coordinate with the seller.
§ 06Contact

For questions about the transaction details or to request additional documents, please contact:
